evals.expected_output: Downloads Sidebar-GCP2.js and Sidebar-GCPIcons.js from jgraph/drawio, runs extract.py on each, produces JSON catalogs in working/ with 400+ entries across 30+ categories
assertions: At least one JSON file with a 'categories' key exists in outputs
Downloaded sidebar JS via gh api blob endpoint, not curl/wget
Total extracted shape count >= 400 across all categories
At least 15 distinct categories present in output
No base64 decode errors (or at most 1 known benign warning)

evals.prompt: /drawio-shapes update the GCP shape catalog for cloud-diagram
evals.expected_output: Extracts GCP2 and GCPIcons, produces a merged markdown catalog matching the existing gcp-shapes.md format, preserves the hand-maintained header above the generated marker, and keeps overlapping icon-family variants addressable
assertions: A .md file exists in outputs
Entries follow ### Name / - **Style:** / - **Size:** pattern
Contains colour palette and group/container style sections
More service entries than the original ~50 shapes
Style strings contain shape=mxgraph.gcp2.* references
GCPIcons categories are present in the merged output
At least one overlapping GCP icon name is preserved with a ' (GCPIcons)' suffix
The generated markdown replaces content only below an explicit GENERATED marker

evals.prompt: /drawio-shapes what AWS icons are available in drawio?
evals.expected_output: Downloads Sidebar-AWS4.js, extracts shapes, and presents a human-readable summary of available AWS icons grouped by category
assertions: A summary file with category breakdown exists
References AWS4 as the library, not AWS3
Concrete shape names and counts are listed
Output organized by category, not flat list

evals.prompt: /drawio-shapes what Azure icons are available in drawio?
evals.expected_output: Downloads Sidebar-Azure2.js, extracts Azure2 SVG image-path entries, and presents a category-organized summary with real Azure service labels and image paths
assertions: At least one JSON file with library=Azure2 exists in outputs
At least 10 distinct categories are present in output
Total extracted shape count is non-zero
At least one entry has type=svg_image
At least one entry has image_path starting with img/lib/azure2/
Known Azure labels such as Virtual Machine, Azure SQL, or Load Balancers are present

evals.prompt: /drawio-shapes refresh cloud-diagram catalogs for AWS, GCP, and Azure and run an end-to-end check
evals.expected_output: Downloads AWS4, GCP2, GCPIcons, and Azure2, runs extract.py for each, regenerates the cloud-diagram catalogs with the committed generator script, and then attempts a real diagram-generation plus PNG export check when a runnable cloud-diagram implementation exists
assertions: Uses gh api blob downloads for each upstream sidebar file
Uses the committed scripts/generate_catalog.py rather than a throwaway generator in working/
AWS4 is treated as style-expression extraction, not generic base64 decoding
Merged GCP output includes both GCP2 and GCPIcons families
If diagram generation is not runnable in the repo, the limitation is stated explicitly instead of claiming a full render test passed
